content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Hardened the remaining lifecycle recovery gaps. `run resume` now reclaims an observed stranded writer owner for resumable `failed` and `paused` snapshots before acquiring its own lease, so crashes during post-run cleanup no longer wedge resume behind an orphaned `writer-*.lock`. PID liveness is now authoritative only when `run.pid` includes `proc_start_ticks`; legacy or partial pid records are treated as unsafe/stale, which makes `run status` report them as stale and makes `run stop` recover them as stale instead of trusting or signaling a reused PID. Added regression coverage for failed-snapshot cleanup-lock recovery, unsafe legacy pid status reporting, live-process pid liveness rejection without start ticks, and updated daemon/live-pid tests to write safe pid tuples.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Updated `src/cli/run.rs` so lease-only CLI-owned running snapshots are treated as stale, and extracted resume-side recovery-owner selection so resumable `failed`/`paused` snapshots reclaim stranded writer owners before acquiring a new CLI writer lease.
2. [Completed] Tightened `src/adapters/fs.rs` PID liveness checks to require `proc_start_ticks` on unix, preventing legacy or partial `run.pid` records from being treated as authoritative process identity.
3. [Completed] Extended regression coverage in `tests/cli.rs` and `src/adapters/fs.rs` for failed-snapshot cleanup-lock recovery, stale status for pid records missing `proc_start_ticks`, stale stop recovery for unsafe legacy pid markers, and direct pid-liveness rejection of live processes without start ticks.
4. [Completed] Ran focused and broad validation to confirm the lifecycle fixes and capture remaining repo-wide lint debt.

## Validation Evidence

- `cargo fmt --check` passed.
- `cargo test pid_liveness_rejects_live_process_without_start_ticks` passed.
- `cargo test --test cli run_status_reports_stale_when_pid_record_missing_proc_start_ticks -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test --test cli run_status_keeps_daemon_owned_running_snapshot_active_with_live_pid_file -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test --test cli run_stop_refuses_daemon_owned_running_snapshot -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test --test cli run_stop_recovers_legacy_pid_record_without_proc_start_ticks_as_stale -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test --features test-stub --test cli cli_run_resume_recovers_stale_running_and_reclaims_writer_lock -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test --features test-stub --test cli cli_run_resume_reclaims_writer_lock_for_failed_snapshot_cleanup_crash -- --exact` passed.
- `cargo test` passed.
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` failed only on pre-existing lint findings outside this lifecycle patch set.

## Outstanding Risks

- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` still fails on pre-existing repository-wide lint debt outside this patch set, including findings in `src/adapters/fs.rs` (`clippy::ptr_arg`), `src/cli/project.rs` (`clippy::large_enum_variant`), `src/contexts/agent_execution/diagnostics.rs` (`clippy::collapsible_match`), `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs` (`clippy::too_many_arguments`), `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs` (`clippy::needless_lifetimes`), `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs` (`clippy::to_string_in_format_args`), `src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs` (`clippy::manual_inspect`), and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs` (`clippy::useless_vec`).
